meta.name: Caribbean Brown Stew Fish
meta.subtitle: Jamaican-style browned fish in a savory sweet-brown gravy with peppers, onion, and thyme

source.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
source.attribution: Modeled from common Jamaican brown stew fish patterns: citrus-seasoned fish, light fry for color, then simmered in a browned gravy with aromatics and thyme.

yield.notes: Assumes medium fillets or steaks. Whole small fish also works with adjusted timing.

ingredients.label: Firm white fish (snapper, kingfish, tilapia, whiting) fillets or steaks
ingredients.notes: Snapper/kingfish are classic; tilapia/whiting are common home options.

ingredients.label: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: For seasoning and that Caribbean bright note.

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste and fish thickness.

ingredients.label: Tomato (sliced) or diced tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Some homes use a little tomato; others skip. Diced tomatoes: 1/2 cup.

ingredients.label: Browning sauce
ingredients.notes: Optional. If you don't have it, rely on caramelized sugar/veg + light fry for color.

ingredients.label: Fish stock, chicken stock, or water
ingredients.notes: Use stock if available; water works fine.

ingredients.label: Soy sauce (or tamari)
ingredients.notes: Optional. Adds savory depth. Use tamari for GF.

ingredients.label: White vinegar or apple cider vinegar
Scotch bonnet (whole)
ingredients.notes: Keep whole for aroma. Do not burst unless you want it hotter.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil (for frying)
ingredients.notes: Enough to shallow fry fish for browning.

ingredients.label: Flour (or cornstarch) for light dredge
ingredients.notes: Optional. Light dredge helps crisp and thickens gravy slightly. Use cornstarch for gluten-free.

steps.title: Season the fish
instructions: Pat fish dry.
Season with lime juice, salt, black pepper, half the garlic, half the scallion, and thyme (rub gently).
Rest 10–15 minutes while you prep vegetables.

steps.title: Brown the fish (light fry)
instructions: Optional: lightly dredge fish in flour or cornstarch; shake off excess.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high.
Fry fish in batches 2–3 minutes per side until lightly browned (not cooked through).
Remove to a plate. Reduce heat to medium.

steps.title: Build the brown stew gravy
instructions: In the same skillet, pour off excess oil leaving ~1–2 tbsp.
Add onion, bell pepper, and optional carrot; saute 3–4 minutes until softened.
Stir in remaining garlic (and optional ginger). Cook 30 seconds.
Add brown sugar and stir 20–30 seconds to caramelize slightly (watch closely).
Stir in optional tomato paste for 30 seconds.
Add stock/water, optional soy sauce, and optional browning sauce. Bring to a simmer.
Add tomato and whole scotch bonnet (if using).

steps.title: Simmer fish in the gravy
instructions: Nestle browned fish into the simmering gravy (spoon gravy over top).
Cover loosely (or partially cover) and simmer 8–12 minutes depending on thickness, until fish flakes easily.
If gravy is too thin, simmer uncovered 2–3 minutes to reduce. If too thick, add a splash of water/stock.

steps.title: Finish and serve
instructions: Taste gravy and adjust salt/pepper. Optional: add a tiny splash of vinegar for brightness.
Remove whole scotch bonnet before serving (unless you intentionally burst it).
Serve with rice and peas, boiled green bananas/yam, or fried plantains. Spoon gravy over everything.

adapt.notes: Skip flour dredge or use cornstarch. Use tamari instead of soy sauce.
Skip scotch bonnet or keep it whole and remove early. Use sweet bell pepper for flavor.
Score whole fish, season well, fry slightly longer per side for color, then simmer until done (often 12–18 minutes depending on size).

fridge.notes: Fish is best within 1–2 days. Reheat gently to avoid overcooking.

freezer.notes: Not recommended—fish texture suffers after freezing in gravy.

reheat.notes: Reheat gently. Add a splash of water/stock if gravy thickens.

batchPlanHints.notes: Best made same-day, but leftovers reheat well if warmed gently.
